content: Curiosity-driven reinforcement learning methods like ICM and RND can be reframed as a recursive self-improvement loop for the general agent itself, where the agent’s ongoing comparison of its own exploratory performance across intrinsic reward methods acts as an intrinsic reward signal for refining its own memory and active learning capabilities. This recursive self-comparison addresses a key flaw of current curiosity methods: their tendency to get stuck in noisy, uninformative state loops by anchoring exploration to the agent’s own improvement in distinguishing useful novelty from random noise.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
